# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent datastore security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: datastore_security

apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: elasticsearch-snapshot
spec:
  version: 7.10.0
  nodeSets:
    - name: default
      count: 3
      config:
        discovery.type: single-node
        xpack.security.enabled: true
        xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
        xpack.security.transport.ssl.keystore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/elastic-certificates.p12
        xpack.security.transport.ssl.truststore.path: /etc/elasticsearch/elastic-certificates.p12
        xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
        xpack.snapshotting.enabled: true
  snapshotRepository:
    name: s3-repository
    type: s3
    settings:
      bucket: my-snapshot-bucket
      region: us-east-1
      access_key_id: ${ACCESS_KEY_ID}
      secret_access_key: ${SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}
      endpoint: https://s3.amazonaws.com
  snapshotSchedule:
    name: daily-backup
    schedule: "0 2 * * *" # Daily at 2 AM
    repository: s3-repository
    indices: "*"
    ignoreUnavailable: true
    includeGlobalState: false
